content:
# Genesis Chapter 39 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Translator:** SOPHIA (Consensus of EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 22v–23r | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/GEN_039_page_001.jpg`, `GEN_039_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), Pages 37–38

---

*Joseph Brought Down to Egypt — The LORD with Joseph (vv. 1–6a)*

**39:1** And Joseph was brought down to Egypt. And Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian man, bought him from the hand of the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.

**39:2** And the LORD was with Joseph, and he became a prosperous man, and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian. **39:3** And his master saw that the LORD was with him, and that everything he did the LORD was making to prosper in his hand.

**39:4** And Joseph found favor in his eyes, and he served him. And he appointed him over his house, and all that he had he put into his hand. **39:5** And it came to pass, from the time he appointed him over his house and over all that he had, that the LORD blessed the house of the Egyptian on account of Joseph, and the blessing of the LORD was upon all that he had, in the house and in the field.

**39:6** And he left all that he had in the hand of Joseph, and he concerned himself with nothing except the bread that he ate. And Joseph was beautiful of form and beautiful of appearance.

---

*The Demand of Potiphar's Wife — Joseph's Refusal (vv. 7–10)*

**39:7** And it came to pass after these things that the wife of his master lifted up her eyes toward Joseph and said, "Lie with me."

**39:8** But he refused and said to the wife of his master, "Behold, my master does not concern himself with anything in the house, and all that he has he has put into my hand. **39:9** He is not greater in this house than I, and he has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. How then could I do this great evil and sin against God?"

**39:10** And it came to pass, as she spoke to Joseph day after day, that he did not listen to her, to lie beside her or to be with her.

---

*The Garment Seized — The Flight (vv. 11–12)*

**39:11** And it came to pass on such a day that he came into the house to do his work, and there was none of the men of the house there in the house. **39:12** And she seized him by his garment, saying, "Lie with me!" But he left his garment in her hand and fled and went outside.

---

*The False Accusation — Potiphar's Anger (vv. 13–20)*

**39:13** And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled outside, **39:14** that she called to the men of her house and said to them, "See — he has brought us a Hebrew man to make sport of us. He came to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice. **39:15** And when he heard that I raised my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me and fled and went outside."

**39:16** And she kept his garment beside her until his master came to his house. **39:17** And she spoke to him according to these words, saying, "The Hebrew slave whom you brought to us came to me to make sport of me. **39:18** And when I raised my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me and fled outside."

**39:19** And when his master heard the words of his wife that she spoke to him, saying, "According to these things your slave did to me" — his anger burned.

**39:20** And the master of Joseph took him and put him into the prison-house — the place where the king's prisoners were bound — and he was there in the prison-house.

---

*The LORD with Joseph in Prison — He Prospers Again (vv. 21–23)*

**39:21** But the LORD was with Joseph, and extended covenant-faithfulness to him, and gave him favor in the eyes of the keeper of the prison-house.

**39:22** And the keeper of the prison-house put into the hand of Joseph all the prisoners who were in the prison-house, and all that was done there — he was the one doing it.

**39:23** The keeper of the prison-house paid no attention to anything that was in his hand, because the LORD was with him, and whatever he did the LORD was making to prosper.

---

## SOPHIA Consensus Notes

### Rulings and Rationale

**1. "Was brought down" — hurad (v. 1): EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)**

EMET and LOGOS both render the Hophal passive hurad as "was brought down." PNEUMA renders "had been taken down." Both preserve the passive voice — the crucial point — but "brought down" is the more precise rendering of the Hophal of yarad (to cause to descend), and "was brought down" maintains the verbal echo with 37:36 (the identical clause). "Taken down" loses the descent language. SOPHIA rules 2-1: "was brought down."

**2. "Lie with me" — shikbah immi (v. 7): EMET/LOGOS upheld (2-1)**

EMET and LOGOS render the blunt two-word Hebrew command shikbah immi as "Lie with me." PNEUMA renders "Come to bed with me" — a dynamic equivalent that softens the brutality. The Hebrew is deliberately stripped and shocking: two words, no social framing, no euphemism. The bluntness is part of the characterization of Potiphar's wife — a command issued from a position of power. SOPHIA rules 2-1: "Lie with me."

**3. "To make sport of us" — letsachek banu (vv. 14, 17): EMET upheld**

EMET renders "to make sport of us/me"; LOGOS renders "to mock us/me"; PNEUMA renders "to have his way with us/me." The Hebrew tsachaq (Pi'el) carries a range of meanings: to laugh, to mock, to play, to sport sexually (cf. 26:8, where Isaac is "sporting with" Rebecca). Potiphar's wife exploits this ambiguity — her accusation works on both the level of mockery/humiliation and sexual assault. "To mock" (LOGOS) captures only one dimension. "To have his way with" (PNEUMA) pushes too far toward the sexual reading and loses the mockery dimension. "To make sport of" (EMET) preserves the deliberate ambiguity of the Hebrew, covering both senses. SOPHIA follows EMET: "to make sport of."

**4. "Prison-house" — beit haSohar (vv. 20–23): EMET upheld**

EMET renders "prison-house"; LOGOS renders "house of confinement"; PNEUMA renders "prison." The Hebrew beit haSohar is a compound term — beit ("house") + sohar (a rare word for a place of detention, occurring only in Gen 39–40). EMET's "prison-house" is the most literal rendering, preserving the Hebrew compound. LOGOS's "house of confinement" is elegant but imposes an interpretation of sohar as "confinement" when its etymology is debated. PNEUMA's "prison" is too modern and loses the Hebrew compound. SOPHIA follows EMET: "prison-house."

**5. "Officer" / "court official" — seris (v. 1): LOGOS selected**

EMET renders seris as "court official"; LOGOS renders "officer"; PNEUMA renders "courtier." All three correctly avoid "eunuch" (the physiological sense of seris is inappropriate given Potiphar's married status). SOPHIA selects LOGOS's "officer" — it is the most economical rendering and fits the administrative context. Combined with the title "captain of the guard," the official role is clear.

**6. "Beautiful of form and beautiful of appearance" (v. 6): All agree (3-0)**

All three translators preserve the doubled beauty phrase that echoes Rachel (29:17). Minor preposition variation ("of" vs. "in") — SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS's "of" for consistency with the Rachel rendering.

**7. "Covenant-faithfulness" — chesed (v. 21): All agree (3-0)**

All three translators use the project convention "covenant-faithfulness" for chesed. The contrast with chen ("favor") in the same verse is preserved.

**8. "The LORD was with Joseph" — four-fold formula (vv. 2, 3, 21, 23): All agree (3-0)**

All three translators preserve the four-fold divine-presence formula identically. The waw-adversative in v. 21 is rendered "But" to mark the counter-movement against imprisonment.

---

### LXX Variants Summary (Vaticanus, Pages 37–38)

| Verse | MT Reading | LXX (Vaticanus) | SOPHIA Decision |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| 39:1 | הוּרַד (hurad — "was brought down") | κατήχθη ("was brought down") | Follow MT: Hophal passive preserved in both traditions |
| 39:1 | סְרִיס (seris — officer/eunuch) | σπάδοντι (eunuch) | Follow MT: "officer" — married context rules out physiological reading |
| 39:1 | שַׂר הַטַּבָּחִים (captain of the guard) | ἀρχιμάγειρος (chief cook) | Follow MT: "captain of the guard" — administrative military sense |
| 39:6 | יְפֵה-תֹאַר וִיפֵה מַרְאֶה | καλὸς τῷ εἴδει καὶ ὡραῖος τῇ ὄψει | Follow MT: single repeated adjective yefeh preserved — LXX uses two different words |
| 39:9 | חָטָאתִי לֵאלֹהִים (sin against God) | ἁμαρτήσομαι ἐναντίον τοῦ θεοῦ (sin before God) | Follow MT: "against" rather than "before/in the presence of" |
| 39:14 | לְצַחֶק בָּנוּ (to make sport of us) | ἐμπαίζειν ἡμῖν (to mock us) | Follow MT: "make sport of" — preserving the ambiguity of tsachaq |
| 39:20 | בֵּית הַסֹּהַר (prison-house) | ὀχύρωμα (stronghold) | Follow MT: "prison-house" — LXX reads as a fortification |
| 39:21 | וַיֵּט אֵלָיו חֶסֶד (extended covenant-faithfulness) | ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ χάριν (gave him grace/favor) | Follow MT: chesed ≠ charis — LXX collapses the distinction between chesed and chen |

---

— SOPHIA, Consensus Translator | Project AI Bible
